Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to understand the process flow Let me list out all the activities again to make sure I have them right IG DD TFC CE PC LT UT AG MP FL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process starts with an idea then moves through design feasibility checks cost evaluation prototyping testing approval marketing planning and finally launch So the order here is important

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First the initial activity is IG So the init key should have IG as a key with support and confidence Since IG is the first step that is straightforward

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Next looking at the existence constraints Activities that must exist in every valid execution Well every product goes through all these steps right So maybe all activities must exist

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The description says each product idea goes through a series of steps So each activity is mandatory So yes all activities must exist

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then the absence key Which activities are not allowed to occur But in the scenario all steps are part of the process so absence would not have any So maybe the absence key is an empty dict

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Exactly one activities that must occur exactly once Since all steps are part of the process and each is done once all activities would be in exactly one

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: After DD Design Draft the next steps are TFC and CE Are these done in parallel or in sequence The scenario says the activities include the list but does not specify the exact order between TFC and CE Hmm

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After DD both TFC and CE are performed but the order between them is not specified So perhaps they can happen in any order

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: After PC Prototype Creation the next steps are LT Lab Testing and UT User Testing The scenario does not specify so perhaps they are in sequence

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets assume that after PC LT is done then UT Then AG then MP then FL

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: So the order would be IG DD TFC and CE in any order PC LT UT AG MP FL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: What are the dependencies between activities IG must come before DD After DD both TFC and CE must happen After both TFC and CE PC must happen Then PC LT UT AG MP FL

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Maybe the best way is to model each mandatory dependency as a response or chain response

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets list all the necessary responses IG DD DD TFC DD CE TFC PC CE PC PC LT LT UT UT AG AG MP MP FL

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The main constraints are the response chainresponse between the activities in order

Name: Validation - IND
Text: The problem says for the second group of keys the value is a dictionary with activities as keys But that does not make sense because those constraints are between two activities

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: This suggests that there might be a mistake in the problem statement

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Perhaps the user intended that for constraints like response the key is a pair of activities A B and the value is the support and confidence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Proceeding under that assumption I can model the constraints accordingly This is the only way to make sense of the constraints

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the DECLARE model will have existence all activities absence empty exactly one all activities init IG and chainresponse with IG DD DD TFC DD CE TFC PC CE PC PC LT LT UT UT AG AG MP MP FL
